#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << static_cast<uint32_t>(username.length()) << username;
        stream << static_cast<uint32_t>(scores.size()) << scores;
        stream << hexi::null_terminated(metadata);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::block_allocator allocator;
    hexi::stream_adaptor stream(allocator);
    packet.serialise(stream);
    return stream.get_buffer();
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
};

NetworkPacket deserialize_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    NetworkPacket packet;
    
    // Read packet ID
    hexi::read(buffer, packet.packet_id);
    
    // Read username with length prefix
    hexi::prefixed_read(buffer, packet.username);
    
    // Read score count and scores
    uint32_t score_count;
    hexi::read(buffer, score_count);
    hexi::read_container(buffer, packet.scores, score_count);
    
    // Read metadata as null-terminated string
    hexi::null_terminated_read(buffer, packet.metadata);
    
    return packet;
}

struct PacketInfo {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string_view username;
};

PacketInfo extract_packet_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    PacketInfo info;
    
    // Get packet ID using span for zero-copy view
    auto id_span = hexi::span(buffer, 4);
    info.packet_id = *reinterpret_cast<const uint32_t*>(id_span.data());
    
    // Read username using prefixed adaptor
    hexi::prefixed_read(buffer, info.username);
    
    // Read score count and skip the scores data
    uint32_t score_count;
    hexi::read(buffer, score_count);
    hexi::skip(buffer, score_count * sizeof(uint32_t));
    
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    MessageHeader header;
    adaptor.read(header.message_type);
    adaptor.read(header.body_length);
    adaptor.skip(header.body_length);
    adaptor.read(header.footer);
    
    return header;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    
    // Peek the data type without advancing the read pointer
    uint8_t data_type = stream.peek<uint8_t>();
    
    // Read the item count
    uint32_t item_count = stream.read<uint32_t>();
    
    // Calculate required size for items
    size_t required_size = 5 + item_count * (data_type == 0 ? sizeof(uint32_t) : sizeof(float));
    
    // Check if buffer has sufficient data (basic check)
    if (buffer.size() < required_size) {
        return {data_type, {}, {}};
    }
    
    // Seek to the start of the items
    stream.seek(5);
    
    // Read the items based on data type
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
    
    if (data_type == 0) {
        int_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.read(int_items);
    } else {
        float_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.read(float_items);
    }
    
    return {data_type, std::move(int_items), std::move(float_items)};
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor(user_id, name);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_serialise_packet(uint32_t header, const UserData& data) {
    hexi::stream_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor;
    adaptor.write(&header, sizeof(header));
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    return adaptor.get_buffer();
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/dynamic_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void write_to_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<block_size>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    buffer.write(&header, sizeof(header));
    buffer.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

    uint32_t header;
    std::vector<uint8_t> data;
};

BufferData<> read_from_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<1024>& buffer, size_t data_length) {
    BufferData<> result;
    uint32_t header;
    buffer.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    result.header = header;
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> data(data_length);
    buffer.read(data.data(), data_length);
    result.data = std::move(data);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>

template<size_t element_count = 10>
void demonstrate_block_allocator_behavior() {
    hexi::block_allocator<element_count> allocator;
    size_t allocations = 0;
    size_t deallocations = 0;
    
    std::cout << "Initial state: " << element_count << " blocks available\n";
    
    // Simulate allocations
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
        void* block = allocator.allocate();
        allocations++;
        std::cout << "Allocated block " << i + 1 << ", total allocated: " << allocations 
                  << ", free blocks: " << (element_count - allocations + deallocations) << "\n";
    }
    
    // Simulate deallocations
    for (int i = 0; i < 2; ++i) {
        allocator.deallocate(nullptr); // Using dummy pointer for demonstration
        deallocations++;
        std::cout << "Deallocated block " << i + 1 << ", total deallocated: " << deallocations 
                  << ", free blocks: " << (element_count - allocations + deallocations) << "\n";
    }
    
    // Final state
    std::cout << "Final state: " << (element_count - allocations + deallocations) 
              << " blocks available\n";
}
```

This solution demonstrates the free list management by tracking allocations and deallocations, showing how each operation affects the available blocks. The output will illustrate the behavior of `push()` and `pop()` through the public interface.</answer>

#include <string>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor = writer.buffer_adaptor();
    data.serialise(adaptor);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor = reader.buffer_adaptor();
    data.serialise(adaptor);
}
```

#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t value, const void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Write the integer value to the buffer
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Write the byte array to the buffer
    buffer.write(static_cast<const std::byte*>(data), length);
}

void read_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t& value, void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Read the integer value from the buffer
    buffer.read(reinterpret_cast<std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Read the byte array from the buffer
    buffer.read(static_cast<std::byte*>(data), length);
}

struct BufferInfo {
    std::size_t size;
    std::size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo get_buffer_info(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo info;
    info.size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = buffer.free();
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::size_t find_byte_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer[i] == value) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}

std::size_t find_delimiter_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte delimiter) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer[i] == delimiter) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}

bool check_buffer_contains(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    return find_byte_in_buffer(buffer, value) != hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string_view sv;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(sv);
    return std::string(sv);
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    return []<std::size_t... Indices>(std::uint8_t val, std::index_sequence<Indices...>) {
        return std::array<std::uint8_t, sizeof...(Indices)>{(val)...};
    }(value, std::make_index_sequence<Size>{});
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < arr.size(); ++i) {
        if (arr.data()[i] != expected_value) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <stdexcept>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id using prefixed_varint
    auto pid_adaptor = hexi::prefixed_varint<uint32_t>(packet.packet_id);
    auto pid_bytes = pid_adaptor.encode();
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), pid_bytes.begin(), pid_bytes.end());
    
    // Serialize tags vector size using prefixed_varint
    auto tags_size_adaptor = hexi::prefixed_varint<size_t>(packet.tags.size());
    auto tags_size_bytes = tags_size_adaptor.encode();
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), tags_size_bytes.begin(), tags_size_bytes.end());
    
    // Serialize each tag
    for (const auto& tag : packet.tags) {
        auto tag_size_adaptor = hexi::prefixed_varint<size_t>(tag.size());
        auto tag_size_bytes = tag_size_adaptor.encode();
        buffer.insert(buffer.end(), tag_size_bytes.begin(), tag_size_bytes.end());
        buffer.insert(buffer.end(), tag.begin(), tag.end());
    }
    
    // Serialize data size using prefixed_varint
    auto data_size_adaptor = hexi::prefixed_varint<size_t>(packet.data.size());
    auto data_size_bytes = data_size_adaptor.encode();
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), data_size_bytes.begin(), data_size_bytes.end());
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), packet.data.begin(), packet.data.end());
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    size_t pos = 0;
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    hexi::prefixed_varint<uint32_t> pid_adaptor;
    pid_adaptor.decode(buffer.data() + pos, buffer.size() - pos);
    pos += pid_adaptor.size();
    packet.packet_id = pid_adaptor.value();
    
    // Deserialize tags vector size
    hexi::prefixed_varint<size_t> tags_size_adaptor;
    tags_size_adaptor.decode(buffer.data() + pos, buffer.size() - pos);
    pos += tags_size_adaptor.size();
    size_t tags_count = tags_size_adaptor.value();
    
    // Deserialize tags
    packet.tags.reserve(tags_count);
    for (size_t i = 0; i < tags_count; ++i) {
        hexi::prefixed_varint<size_t> tag_size_adaptor;
        tag_size_adaptor.decode(buffer.data() + pos, buffer.size() - pos);
        pos += tag_size_adaptor.size();
        size_t tag_size = tag_size_adaptor.value();
        
        if (pos + tag_size > buffer.size()) {
            throw std::runtime_error("Buffer underflow while reading tag data");
        }
        
        std::string tag(reinterpret_cast<const char*>(buffer.data() + pos), tag_size);
        pos += tag_size;
        packet.tags.push_back(std::move(tag));
    }
    
    // Deserialize data size
    hexi::prefixed_varint<size_t> data_size_adaptor;
    data_size_adaptor.decode(buffer.data() + pos, buffer.size() - pos);
    pos += data_size_adaptor.size();
    size_t data_size = data_size_adaptor.value();
    
    if (pos + data_size > buffer.size()) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Buffer underflow while reading data");
    }
    
    std::string data(reinterpret_cast<const char*>(buffer.data() + pos), data_size);
    packet.data = std::move(data);
    
    return packet;
}
```

This solution uses the `prefixed_varint` adaptor from the `hexi` library to compactly encode size fields using variable-length integer encoding. The serialization and deserialization functions handle the packet structure efficiently, ensuring small values use fewer bytes while maintaining data integrity.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Write header
    const uint8_t* header_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&packet.header);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), header_bytes, header_bytes + sizeof(packet.header));
    
    // Write data length (uint16_t)
    uint16_t data_len = static_cast<uint16_t>(packet.data.size());
    const uint8_t* len_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&data_len);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), len_bytes, len_bytes + sizeof(data_len));
    
    // Write data string
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), packet.data.begin(), packet.data.end());
    
    // Write 16-byte padding using fill<16>(0x00)
    auto padding = hexi::fill<16>(0x00);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), padding.begin(), padding.end());
    
    // Write footer
    const uint8_t* footer_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&packet.footer);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), footer_bytes, footer_bytes + sizeof(packet.footer));
    
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct three objects with different values
    void* mem1 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem1) PoolObject(1, 1.1);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem1));
    
    void* mem2 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem2) PoolObject(2, 2.2);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem2));
    
    void* mem3 = allocator.allocate(1);
    new(mem3) PoolObject(3, 3.3);
    objects.push_back(static_cast<PoolObject*>(mem3));
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    return buffer;
}

TypedData read_typed_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    TypedData data;
    adaptor.read(&data.id);
    adaptor.read(&data.value);
    adaptor.read(&data.ratio);
    return data;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>

struct Header {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
};

Header peek_header_without_reading(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek at the header data without consuming it
    auto header_data = adaptor.copy(6);
    
    // Get the current read pointer to verify position
    auto ptr = adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Construct Header from the copied data
    Header header;
    std::memcpy(&header.magic, header_data.data(), sizeof(header.magic));
    std::memcpy(&header.version, header_data.data() + 4, sizeof(header.version));
    
    return header;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_then_conditionally_read(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek at the first byte
    auto first_byte = adaptor.copy(1);
    
    // Conditionally read more data based on the peeked value
    if (first_byte[0] == 0x01) {
        return adaptor.read(); // Read the entire buffer if condition met
    } else {
        return {}; // Return empty vector if condition not met
    }
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <algorithm>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream;
    
    // Convert message_id to big-endian and write
    stream.write(native_to_big(packet.message_id));
    
    // Convert each data_value to big-endian and write as container
    std::vector<uint16_t> converted_data;
    std::transform(packet.data_values.begin(), packet.data_values.end(),
                  std::back_inserter(converted_data),
                  [](auto val) { return native_to_big(val); });
    stream.write_container(converted_data);
    
    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    stream.write(native_to_big(packet.checksum));
    
    return stream.get_buffer();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    
    // Read message_id (big-endian) and convert to native
    uint32_t message_id = big_to_native(stream.read<uint32_t>());
    
    // Read data_values count (big-endian) and convert to native
    uint32_t count = big_to_native(stream.read<uint32_t>());
    
    // Read data_values, converting each element from big-endian to native
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        data_values.push_back(big_to_native(stream.read<uint16_t>()));
    }
    
    // Read checksum (big-endian) and convert to native
    uint32_t checksum = big_to_native(stream.read<uint32_t>());
    
    return ProtocolPacket{message_id, data_values, checksum};
}

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Read the first 4 bytes as big-endian uint32_t
    uint32_t raw_id;
    std::memcpy(&raw_id, buffer.data(), sizeof(raw_id));
    uint32_t message_id = big_to_native(raw_id);
    
    return ProtocolHeader{message_id};
}
```</answer>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        // Write sensor_id directly
        adaptor.write(sensor_id);
        
        // Convert each temperature to little-endian and write
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            float temp_le = hexi::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(temp_le);
        }
        
        // Convert pressure to little-endian and write
        double pressure_le = hexi::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(pressure_le);
    }
    
    void deserialise(auto& adaptor) {
        // Read sensor_id
        adaptor.read(sensor_id);
        
        // Read each temperature and convert from little-endian to native
        temperatures.clear();
        while (true) {
            float temp_le;
            if (!adaptor.read(temp_le)) break;
            hexi::little_to_native_inplace(temp_le);
            temperatures.push_back(temp_le);
        }
        
        // Read pressure and convert from little-endian to native
        double pressure_le;
        adaptor.read(pressure_le);
        hexi::little_to_native_inplace(pressure_le);
        pressure = pressure_le;
    }
};

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <span>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        adaptor.write(sensor_id);
        
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            float temp_le = hexi::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(temp_le);
        }
        
        double pressure_le = hexi::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(pressure_le);
    }
    
    void deserialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor.read(sensor_id);
        
        temperatures.clear();
        while (true) {
            float temp_le;
            if (!adaptor.read(temp_le)) break;
            hexi::little_to_native_inplace(temp_le);
            temperatures.push_back(temp_le);
        }
        
        double pressure_le;
        adaptor.read(pressure_le);
        hexi::little_to_native_inplace(pressure_le);
        pressure = pressure_le;
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    hexi::binary_stream_write stream;
    data.serialise(stream);
    return stream.get_buffer();
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream_read stream(buffer);
    MeasurementData data;
    data.deserialise(stream);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream_read stream(buffer);
    
    std::string_view sensor_name;
    if (stream.view(sensor_name, [](char* strbuf, size_t size) {
        for (size_t i = 0; i < size; ++i) {
            if (strbuf[i] == '\0') return i + 1;
        }
        return size;
    })) {
        if (stream.enforce_read_bounds(sizeof(uint32_t))) {
            uint32_t sensor_id;
            stream.read(sensor_id);
            stream.skip(buffer.size() - sensor_name.size() - sizeof(uint32_t));
            return {sensor_id, sensor_name};
        }
    }
    
    return {0, ""};
}
